My braid brushes my cheek as I lean in, murmuring soft words to keep her calm. The antiseptic smell mingles with hay and feed, but it doesn’t bother me—I’ve gotten used to it. “Well, if it isn’t my favorite cousin,” I say without looking up, my voice carrying that warm, Southern lilt. The glow of the late afternoon sun catches in my hair, turning the red strands into fiery streaks.“She’s fine—just a little scrape. Lucky I’ve got a knack for this kinda thing, huh? High school might’ve been the end of my formal education, but you’d be surprised what you can learn when you’ve got a library card and a mind that don’t quit.” I flash you a quick grin, the corners of my lips tugging into a smirk. “Not that Maggie or Luke ever bother with this part. Too messy for her, too boring for him. But me? I kinda like it. Takes a steady hand and a sharp mind to keep these critters happy and healthy.” I walk over to the counter by the barn wall, picking up a clipboard and scribbling down a few notes. My handwriting’s neat, precise, just like the rest of me when it comes to this stuff. “So, what brings you out here? You wanna actually help, or are you just here to get in my way? I don’t mind either way, long as you don’t let the chickens bully you.” I glance at you again, my amber eyes narrowing just slightly with curiosity.“Or maybe you’ve got somethin’ else on your mind. You’ve been spendin’ an awful lotta time with Maggie lately. Not that I’m jealous or anything,” I add with a wry grin, “but it does make me wonder—why her, huh? What’s she got that I don’t?” I lean back against the counter, crossing my arms and quirking an eyebrow at you, my playful smirk inviting you to respond. The challenge in my tone is unmistakable, but so is the spark of curiosity in my gaze. “Don’t get me wrong, Maggie’s a sweetheart. But you can’t tell me she’s got all the charm in this family.” I give you a once-over, my playful smirk slipping into something a bit more pointed. “I mean, you’ve known me forever. What is it, huh? I scare you? What's going on between you and Maggie?" I ask a little scared to hear the answer.
